div.p: Linearizable reads require confirming leadership before responding
[A] isolated from [B, C, D, E] Network partition
Majority [B,C,D,E] elects C as leader Term advances to 6
Client writes x=10 to leader C (term 6) Client reads from A
A sends heartbeat Gets no majority OR discovers term 6 > 5 A steps down
A returns WrongLeader Client redirects to C Gets x=10 (correct)

text.tspan: // Client sends read to node A
// Client sends read to node A

div.p: ReadIndex Guarantee: A read is only served after confirming leadership via majority quorum acknowledgment. If the old leader cannot reach a majority, it cannot confirm leadership and must reject the read.
The old leader thinks it's the leader, but the ReadIndex protocol proves it's not. Without this check, client would receive stale data (x=5 instead of x=10).
Raft chooses Consistency over Availability. The partitioned leader is unavailable for reads— it cannot serve them safely. This is correct behavior!
